mcqs.question: Which GLUT function creates a new pop-up menu and returns its identifier?
mcqs.explanation: glutCreateMenu() is the correct function. It creates a new pop-up menu and returns a unique integer identifier (menu ID). Other options are not valid GLUT functions.

mcqs.question: What is the return type of glutCreateMenu()?
mcqs.explanation: glutCreateMenu() returns an unsigned int (or int in practice) that serves as a unique identifier for the created menu. This ID is used in other menu-related functions.

mcqs.question: Which mouse button is commonly used to trigger a pop-up menu in GLUT applications?
mcqs.explanation: GLUT_RIGHT_BUTTON is conventionally used for triggering pop-up menus (context menus) in most applications, following the standard GUI convention where right-click opens context-sensitive options.

mcqs.question: What does glutAttachMenu(int button) do?

options.text: Attaches the current menu to a mouse button for activation
Creates a button on the menu window

mcqs.explanation: glutAttachMenu() attaches the menu created with glutCreateMenu() to a specified mouse button. When that button is clicked, the menu pops up at the cursor location.

mcqs.question: What is the difference between glutAddMenuEntry() and glutAddSubMenu()?

options.text: They are identical functions with different names
MenuEntry adds a clickable item; SubMenu adds a cascading submenu
MenuEntry is for text; SubMenu is for graphics
MenuEntry requires two parameters; SubMenu requires one

mcqs.explanation: glutAddMenuEntry() adds a menu item that executes a callback when selected. glutAddSubMenu() adds a menu item that, when selected, reveals another menu (cascading menu).

mcqs.question: What parameter is passed to the menu callback function in GLUT?
mcqs.explanation: When a menu item is selected, GLUT calls the callback function with a single integer argument - the value associated with that menu item when it was created.

mcqs.question: Which function is used to remove a menu from a mouse button in GLUT?
mcqs.explanation: glutDetachMenu(int button) removes the menu attached to the specified mouse button. This is useful for temporarily disabling menus or reassigning button functionality.

mcqs.question: What is the correct order of operations to create a GLUT menu with submenu?

options.text: Create submenu → Attach to button → Create main menu → Add entries
Create main menu → Create submenu → Add items to submenu → Add submenu to main menu → Attach to button
Attach to button → Create main menu → Create submenu → Add entries
Create submenu → Add items → Create main menu → Add submenu to main menu → Attach

mcqs.explanation: The correct order is: 1) Create submenu first (glutCreateMenu), 2) Add items to submenu, 3) Create main menu (glutCreateMenu), 4) Add submenu to main menu (glutAddSubMenu), 5) Attach to button (glutAttachMenu).

mcqs.question: What happens if you call glutCreateMenu() without first creating a glutInit()?

options.text: The program compiles but crashes at runtime
The menu is created with default values
GLUT initializes automatically before menu creation

mcqs.explanation: glutCreateMenu() must be called after glutInit(). If called before, it will cause undefined behavior or runtime errors because the GLUT context is not initialized.

mcqs.question: In a GLUT menu callback function, what would be the appropriate way to handle different menu selections for values 1, 2, and 3?

options.text: Use three separate callback functions registered with each menu entry
Use if-else or switch statement to check the value parameter
Return the value from callback for external processing
Call glutGetMenu() to determine which item was selected

mcqs.explanation: A single callback function receives all menu selections. Using a switch or if-else statement on the value parameter is the standard approach to handle different menu items.

mcqs.question: What is the maximum number of menus that can be created in a GLUT application?
mcqs.explanation: GLUT allows multiple menus to be created (subject to system resource limits). Each menu has a by glutCreateMenu().

mcqs.question: Which GLUT function adds a separator line in a menu?

options.text: There is no separator function in GLUT

mcqs.explanation: GLUT does not provide a built-in function for adding separator lines in menus. Developers must create text-based separators like "-------" or implement custom rendering for visual separation.

mcqs.question: What is a context menu in computer graphics?

options.text: A menu that appears at application startup
A menu that appears at cursor position on right-click, showing context-specific options
A menu attached to a toolbar button
A menu that displays help information

mcqs.explanation: A context menu (or pop-up menu) appears at the current cursor position when triggered, providing context-sensitive options based on what the user is interacting with.

mcqs.question: When should glutDestroyMenu() be called?

options.text: When the menu is no longer needed, typically before program exit
Only when the window is closed

mcqs.explanation: glutDestroyMenu() should be called when the menu is no longer needed to free resources. While not strictly required before exit (OS reclaims memory), it's good practice, especially in long-running applications.

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'value' parameter in glutAddMenuEntry("Draw Point", 1)?

options.text: It specifies the position of the menu item
It is the integer identifier passed to callback when selected
It sets the menu item to be active by default

mcqs.explanation: The value parameter (1 in this example) is the integer that gets passed to the menu callback function when this menu item is selected. The application uses this value to determine which action to perform.
